questions.question: What is the main advantage of using templates in C++?
questions.explanation: Templates allow for generic programming, enabling the creation of reusable and flexible code that can work with different data types.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'template' keyword in C++?
options: To specify the type of a variable
questions.explanation: The 'template' keyword is used to enable generic programming, allowing the creation of reusable and flexible code that can work with different data types.

questions.question: What is the difference between 'template' and 'struct' in C++?
options: Template is used for classes, struct is used for functions
Template is used for functions, struct is used for classes
Template is a type of struct
Template is a type of class
questions.explanation: In C++, 'template' is used to define a class that can work with different data types, whereas 'struct' is used to define a class that is not generic.

questions.question: Can templates be used with built-in types such as int, float, etc.?
options: Yes, but they must be templated with the type
No, they cannot be used with built-in types
Yes, but they must be used without any template parameters
No, templates are only used with user-defined types
questions.explanation: Yes, templates can be used with built-in types such as int, float, etc. They must be templated with the type, e.g., template <typename T> void print(T value).

questions.question: How many template parameters can a template class have?
questions.explanation: A template class can have two or more template parameters, which are used to define the generic type of the class.
